args.content:
<!-- SCOPE
Covers: 9 new consonants (Б, Д, П, З, Г, Х, Ж, Ш, Ч), voiced and voiceless pairs
Not covered:
  - Remaining 10 letters and digraphs → a1-04 (The Cyrillic Code IV)
-->

# The Cyrillic Code III

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are about to unlock nine new consonant letters. Once you finish this lesson, you will know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. This means nearly all common Ukrainian text will become readable to you.

## Вступ — Introduction

Welcome back! Before we dive into new letters, let's warm up by reading a few words you already know from our previous lessons.

Look at these words and read them out loud:
* **молоко́** (milk)
* **кіт** (cat)
* **мі́сто** (city)
* **соба́ка** (dog)
* **вода́** (water)

Excellent job. You are now ready for the next big step in decoding Ukrainian text. Our main objective for this lesson is to introduce nine new consonant letters: **Б**, **Д**, **П**, **З**, **Г**, **Х**, **Ж**, **Ш**, and **Ч**. Adding these to your toolkit unlocks a massive amount of everyday vocabulary. 

As we explore these new consonants, we will also introduce a key concept in Ukrainian pronunciation called voiced and voiceless consonant pairs. This simply means that Ukrainian consonants often come in matched pairs. One letter in the pair vibrates your throat when you speak it, which we call voiced, while its partner only uses air without vibration, which we call voiceless. Understanding this pattern makes pronouncing new words much easier and more natural.

## Приголосні — Consonants Б, Д, П

Let's start with three simple consonants. These shapes might look slightly unfamiliar, but the sounds they make are very straightforward.

### The Letter Б
The letter **Б** makes the sound of the English "b". It is a voiced consonant.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Б](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1hxBE_JbGg)

Here are some examples of words starting with **Б**:
* **бана́н** (banana)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **брат** (brother)
* **банк** (bank)
* **ба́за** (base)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там бана́н.** (There is a banana.)
* **Тут буди́нок.** (Here is a building.)
* **Там брат.** (There is a brother.)

### The Letter Д
The letter **Д** looks a bit like a house sitting on small legs. It makes the sound of the English "d". In Ukrainian, this sound is dental, meaning you place your tongue against the back of your upper teeth, rather than the roof of your mouth.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Д](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4Bh-lqzd48)

Practice the letter **Д** with these words:
* **дім** (house or home)
* **до́бре** (good)
* **друг** (friend)
* **день** (day)
* **дуб** (oak)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там дім.** (There is a home.)
* **Тут друг.** (Here is a friend.)

### The Letter П
The letter **П** looks like a platform or a doorway. It makes the sound of the English "p". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — П](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JksSjjxyW5Y)

There is a very important detail here. In English, when we say a word starting with "p", we usually release a small puff of air. The Ukrainian **П** is unaspirated. This means you should try to say it without that puff of air.

Here are some common words:
* **паву́к** (spider)
* **парк** (park)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **спорт** (sport)
* **суп** (soup)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там парк.** (There is a park.)
* **Тут по́шта.** (Here is a post office.)

> [!warning]
> **Visual Trap Alert!**
> You learned the letter **Р** previously, which looks like an English "P" but sounds like an "r". Now you are learning **П**, which sounds like a "p" but looks like a platform. Do not confuse them!
> Compare **па́лка** (stick) with **раке́та** (rocket). The first word starts with the "p" sound, while the second starts with the "r" sound. 

## Приголосні — Consonants З, Г, Х

Now we will look at three more consonants that require a bit more attention to how they are produced in the mouth and throat.

### The Letter З
The letter **З** looks just like the number three. It makes the sound of the English "z". It is the voiced partner to the letter **С**, which you already know. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — З](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BhASNxitC1A)

Try reading these words:
* **зе́бра** (zebra)
* **зуб** (tooth)
* **звук** (sound)
* **зал** (hall)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там зе́бра.** (There is a zebra.)
* **Тут зал.** (Here is a hall.)

### The Letter Г
The letter **Г** looks like an upside-down L. This letter causes a lot of confusion for beginners, so pay close attention. It does not make a hard "g" sound like in the English word "go". 

Instead, the Ukrainian **Г** is a soft, throaty sound. It is a voiced glottal fricative, sounding a bit closer to a heavy "h". The hard "g" sound does exist in Ukrainian, but it is represented by a different letter, **Ґ**, which we will cover in the next lesson.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Г](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gVnclpSI0DU)

Practice this soft, throaty sound:
* **гора́** (mountain)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **го́лос** (voice)
* **гу́ба** (lip)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там гора́.** (There is a mountain.)
* **Тут газе́та.** (Here is a newspaper.)

### The Letter Х
The letter **Х** looks like the English X, but it makes a completely different sound. It is the voiceless partner of **Г**. 

To pronounce it, you need to create friction in the back of your throat. It sounds similar to the Scottish "loch" or the German "ach". There is no exact English equivalent, so it requires explicit practice. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Х](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpr58zJSJKc)

Read this very important cultural word:
* **хліб** (bread)
* **хо́лод** (cold)
* **ха́та** (house)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Тут хліб.** (Here is bread.)
* **Там ха́та.** (There is a house.)

> [!culture]
> **Bread and Salt**
> The word **хліб** (bread) is central to Ukrainian culture. The tradition of **хліб-сіль** (bread and salt) is an ancient symbol of hospitality. Guests are often welcomed with a loaf of bread topped with salt, showing respect and friendship.

## Дзвінкі та глухі — Voiced and Voiceless Pairs

We have mentioned voiced and voiceless pairs briefly. Now, let's explore this concept practically. 

Place your hand flat against the front of your throat. Say the English sound "z" and hold it: zzzzz. You should feel a strong vibration in your throat. Now, switch to the "s" sound: sssss. The vibration stops, and you only feel air flowing. 

This physical test shows you the difference between a voiced consonant and a voiceless consonant. In Ukrainian, many consonants are organized into these exact pairs. When you learn one, you essentially learn how to form the other by just turning your voice on or off.

Let's learn the final three consonants for this lesson. They are known as sibilants and affricates, which are just technical terms for "hissing" and "buzzing" sounds.

### The Letter Ж
The letter **Ж** looks like a little bug with six legs. It makes a voiced sibilant sound. There is no single letter in English for this, but it sounds like the "s" in "measure" or the "z" in "azure". Keep your lips slightly rounded and your tongue raised.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ж](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dIrGVcqPwqM)

* **жук** (bug)
* **жар** (heat)
* **жа́ба** (frog)
* **журна́л** (magazine)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там жа́ба.** (There is a frog.)
* **Тут журна́л.** (Here is a magazine.)

### The Letter Ш
The letter **Ш** looks like a comb pointing upwards. It is the voiceless partner of **Ж**. It sounds very much like the English "sh" in "ship". When you say it, your tongue is pulled back slightly for a hard articulation.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ш](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1D-6MIw3OXY)

* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **шар** (layer)
* **шум** (noise)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Тут шко́ла.** (Here is a school.)
* **Там ша́пка.** (There is a hat.)

### The Letter Ч
The letter **Ч** looks like an upside-down lower-case "h" or a small chair. It makes the sound of the English "ch" in "church". This is a very frequent sound in Ukrainian.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ч](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UsJkbdsY2RA)

* **черепа́ха** (turtle)
* **чай** (tea)
* **час** (time)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Тут чай.** (Here is tea.)
* **Там черепа́ха.** (There is a turtle.)

> [!tip]
> **Consonant Pairs Summary**
> Let's summarize the pairs you now know. Practice saying them aloud, feeling your throat turn on and off:
> * **Б** (voiced) ↔ **П** (voiceless)
> * **Д** (voiced) ↔ **Т** (voiceless)
> * **Г** (voiced) ↔ **Х** (voiceless)
> * **Ж** (voiced) ↔ **Ш** (voiceless)
> * **З** (voiced) ↔ **С** (voiceless)

## Практика читання — Reading Practice

You have absorbed a lot of new information. The best way to secure it in your memory is to practice reading. We will start with words that are internationalisms. These words sound very similar to their English counterparts, which will build your immediate confidence in recognizing the new letters.

Read these words aloud:
* **банк** (bank)
* **парк** (park)
* **тра́нспорт** (transport)

Next, we will do some discrimination drills. These pairs of words look similar but use different consonants. Pay attention to whether your throat is vibrating or not.

First, let's compare the voiced **З** with the voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) — voiced start
* **суп** (soup) — voiceless start

Now, let's compare the voiced **Ж** with the voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat) — voiced start
* **шар** (layer) — voiceless start

Finally, let's read some highly practical everyday vocabulary. These words use letters from the entire pool of twenty-three characters you have learned so far. Take your time and decode them letter by letter.

* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence of sentences.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
**Там молоко́ і чай.** (There is milk and tea.)
**Тут кіт і соба́ка.** (Here is a cat and a dog.)

You are doing wonderfully. Every word you read makes the letters more familiar to your brain.

## Підсумок — Summary

Congratulations! You have just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three out of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. This includes six vowels and seventeen consonants. You can now read nearly all common text you will encounter in everyday situations. Furthermore, you have mastered the key concept of voiced and voiceless pairs, which will help your pronunciation immensely. 

The next step in your journey will be The Cyrillic Code IV, where we will complete the alphabet by learning the remaining ten letters and special symbols.

**Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform and makes a "p" sound. Р looks like a "P" but makes an "r" sound.
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

---

# The Cyrillic Code III

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are about to unlock nine new consonant letters. Once you finish this lesson, you will know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. This means nearly all common Ukrainian text will become readable to you.

## Вступ — Introduction

Welcome back! Before we dive into new letters, let's warm up by reading a few words you already know from our previous lessons.

Look at these words and read them out loud:
* **молоко́** (milk)
* **кіт** (cat)
* **мі́сто** (city)

Excellent job. You are now ready for the next big step in decoding Ukrainian text. Our main objective for this lesson is to introduce nine new consonant letters: **Б**, **Д**, **П**, **З**, **Г**, **Х**, **Ж**, **Ш**, and **Ч**. Adding these to your toolkit unlocks a massive amount of everyday vocabulary. 

As we explore these new consonants, we will also introduce a key concept in Ukrainian pronunciation called voiced and voiceless consonant pairs. This simply means that Ukrainian consonants often come in matched pairs. One letter in the pair vibrates your throat when you speak it, which we call voiced, while its partner only uses air without vibration, which we call voiceless. Understanding this pattern makes pronouncing new words much easier and more natural.

## Приголосні — Consonants Б, Д, П

Let's start with three simple consonants. These shapes might look slightly unfamiliar, but the sounds they make are very straightforward.

### The Letter Б
The letter **Б** makes the sound of the English "b". It is a voiced consonant.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Б](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1hxBE_JbGg)

Here are some examples of words starting with **Б**:
* **бана́н** (banana)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **брат** (brother)

### The Letter Д
The letter **Д** looks a bit like a house sitting on small legs. It makes the sound of the English "d". In Ukrainian, this sound is dental, meaning you place your tongue against the back of your upper teeth, rather than the roof of your mouth.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Д](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4Bh-lqzd48)

Practice the letter **Д** with these words:
* **дім** (house or home)
* **до́бре** (good)
* **друг** (friend)
* **день** (day)

### The Letter П
The letter **П** looks like a platform or a doorway. It makes the sound of the English "p". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — П](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JksSjjxyW5Y)

There is a very important detail here. In English, when we say a word starting with "p", we usually release a small puff of air. The Ukrainian **П** is unaspirated. This means you should try to say it without that puff of air.

Here are some common words:
* **паву́к** (spider)
* **парк** (park)

> [!warning]
> **Visual Trap Alert!**
> You learned the letter **Р** previously, which looks like an English "P" but sounds like an "r". Now you are learning **П**, which sounds like a "p" but looks like a platform. Do not confuse them!
> Compare **па́лка** (stick) with **раке́та** (rocket). The first word starts with the "p" sound, while the second starts with the "r" sound. 

## Приголосні — Consonants З, Г, Х

Now we will look at three more consonants that require a bit more attention to how they are produced in the mouth and throat.

### The Letter З
The letter **З** looks just like the number three. It makes the sound of the English "z". It is the voiced partner to the letter **С**, which you already know. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — З](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BhASNxitC1A)

Try reading these words:
* **зе́бра** (zebra)
* **зуб** (tooth)

### The Letter Г
The letter **Г** looks like an upside-down L. This letter causes a lot of confusion for beginners, so pay close attention. It does not make a hard "g" sound like in the English word "go". 

Instead, the Ukrainian **Г** is a soft, throaty sound. It is a voiced glottal fricative, sounding a bit closer to a heavy "h". The hard "g" sound does exist in Ukrainian, but it is represented by a different letter, **Ґ**, which we will cover in the next lesson.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Г](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gVnclpSI0DU)

Practice this soft, throaty sound:
* **гора́** (mountain)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)

### The Letter Х
The letter **Х** looks like the English X, but it makes a completely different sound. It is the voiceless partner of **Г**. 

To pronounce it, you need to create friction in the back of your throat. It sounds similar to the Scottish "loch" or the German "ach". There is no exact English equivalent, so it requires explicit practice. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Х](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpr58zJSJKc)

Read this very important cultural word:
* **хліб** (bread)

> [!culture]
> **Bread and Salt**
> The word **хліб** (bread) is central to Ukrainian culture. The tradition of **хліб-сіль** (bread and salt) is an ancient symbol of hospitality. Guests are often welcomed with a loaf of bread topped with salt, showing respect and friendship.

## Дзвінкі та глухі — Voiced and Voiceless Pairs

We have mentioned voiced and voiceless pairs briefly. Now, let's explore this concept practically. 

Place your hand flat against the front of your throat. Say the English sound "z" and hold it: zzzzz. You should feel a strong vibration in your throat. Now, switch to the "s" sound: sssss. The vibration stops, and you only feel air flowing. 

This physical test shows you the difference between a voiced consonant and a voiceless consonant. In Ukrainian, many consonants are organized into these exact pairs. When you learn one, you essentially learn how to form the other by just turning your voice on or off.

Let's learn the final three consonants for this lesson. They are known as sibilants and affricates, which are just technical terms for "hissing" and "buzzing" sounds.

### The Letter Ж
The letter **Ж** looks like a little bug with six legs. It makes a voiced sibilant sound. There is no single letter in English for this, but it sounds like the "s" in "measure" or the "z" in "azure". Keep your lips slightly rounded and your tongue raised.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ж](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dIrGVcqPwqM)

* **жук** (bug)
* **жар** (heat)

### The Letter Ш
The letter **Ш** looks like a comb pointing upwards. It is the voiceless partner of **Ж**. It sounds very much like the English "sh" in "ship". When you say it, your tongue is pulled back slightly for a hard articulation.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ш](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1D-6MIw3OXY)

* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **шко́ла** (school)

### The Letter Ч
The letter **Ч** looks like an upside-down lower-case "h" or a small chair. It makes the sound of the English "ch" in "church". This is a very frequent sound in Ukrainian.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ч](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UsJkbdsY2RA)

* **черепа́ха** (turtle)
* **чай** (tea)

> [!tip]
> **Consonant Pairs Summary**
> Let's summarize the pairs you now know. Practice saying them aloud, feeling your throat turn on and off:
> * **Б** (voiced) ↔ **П** (voiceless)
> * **Д** (voiced) ↔ **Т** (voiceless)
> * **Г** (voiced) ↔ **Х** (voiceless)
> * **Ж** (voiced) ↔ **Ш** (voiceless)
> * **З** (voiced) ↔ **С** (voiceless)

## Практика читання — Reading Practice

You have absorbed a lot of new information. The best way to secure it in your memory is to practice reading. We will start with words that are internationalisms. These words sound very similar to their English counterparts, which will build your immediate confidence in recognizing the new letters.

Read these words aloud:
* **банк** (bank)
* **парк** (park)
* **тра́нспорт** (transport)

Next, we will do some discrimination drills. These pairs of words look similar but use different consonants. Pay attention to whether your throat is vibrating or not.

First, let's compare the voiced **З** with the voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) — voiced start
* **суп** (soup) — voiceless start

Now, let's compare the voiced **Ж** with the voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat) — voiced start
* **шар** (layer) — voiceless start

Finally, let's read some highly practical everyday vocabulary. These words use letters from the entire pool of twenty-three characters you have learned so far. Take your time and decode them letter by letter.

* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

You are doing wonderfully. Every word you read makes the letters more familiar to your brain.

## Підсумок — Summary

Congratulations! You have just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three out of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. This includes six vowels and seventeen consonants. You can now read nearly all common text you will encounter in everyday situations. Furthermore, you have mastered the key concept of voiced and voiceless pairs, which will help your pronunciation immensely. 

The next step in your journey will be The Cyrillic Code IV, where we will complete the alphabet by learning the remaining ten letters and special symbols.

**Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform and makes a "p" sound. Р looks like a "P" but makes an "r" sound.
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

---

Next, we will do some discrimination drills. These pairs of words look similar but use different consonants. Pay attention to whether your throat is vibrating or not.

First, let's compare the voiced **З** with the voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) — voiced start
* **суп** (soup) — voiceless start

Now, let's compare the voiced **Ж** with the voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat) — voiced start
* **шар** (layer) — voiceless start

Finally, let's read some highly practical everyday vocabulary. These words use letters from the entire pool of twenty-three characters you have learned so far. Take your time and decode them letter by letter.

* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence of sentences.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
**Там молоко́ і чай.** (There is milk and tea.)
**Тут кіт і соба́ка.** (Here is a cat and a dog.)

You are doing wonderfully. Every word you read makes the letters more familiar to your brain.

## Підсумок — Summary

Congratulations! You have just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three out of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. This includes six vowels and seventeen consonants. You can now read nearly all common text you will encounter in everyday situations. Furthermore, you have mastered the key concept of voiced and voiceless pairs, which will help your pronunciation immensely. 

The next step in your journey will be The Cyrillic Code IV, where we will complete the alphabet by learning the remaining ten letters and special symbols.

**Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform and makes a "p" sound. Р looks like a "P" but makes an "r" sound.
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

args.content:
<!-- SCOPE
Covers: 9 new consonants (Б, Д, П, З, Г, Х, Ж, Ш, Ч), voiced and voiceless pairs
Not covered:
  - Remaining 10 letters and digraphs → a1-04 (The Cyrillic Code IV)
-->

# The Cyrillic Code III

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are about to unlock nine new consonant letters. Once you finish this lesson, you will know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet.

## Вступ — Introduction

Welcome back! Let's warm up by reading a few words you already know.

Read them out loud:
* **молоко́** (milk)
* **кіт** (cat)
* **мі́сто** (city)
* **соба́ка** (dog)
* **вода́** (water)

Our objective is to introduce nine new consonant letters: **Б**, **Д**, **П**, **З**, **Г**, **Х**, **Ж**, **Ш**, and **Ч**. Adding these unlocks a massive amount of vocabulary. 

We will also introduce voiced and voiceless consonant pairs. One letter vibrates your throat (voiced), while its partner uses only air (voiceless).

## Приголосні — Consonants Б, Д, П

Let's start with three simple consonants. 

### Літера Б — The Letter Б
The letter **Б** makes the sound of the English "b". It is a voiced consonant.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Б](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1hxBE_JbGg)

Examples of words starting with **Б**:
* **бана́н** (banana)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **брат** (brother)
* **банк** (bank)
* **ба́за** (base)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там бана́н.** (There is a banana.)
* **Тут буди́нок.** (Here is a building.)
* **Там брат.** (There is a brother.)

### Літера Д — The Letter Д
The letter **Д** looks a bit like a house. It makes the sound of the English "d". In Ukrainian, this sound is dental (tongue against teeth).

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Д](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4Bh-lqzd48)

Practice the letter **Д**:
* **дім** (house or home)
* **до́бре** (good)
* **друг** (friend)
* **день** (day)
* **дуб** (oak)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там дім.** (There is a home.)
* **Тут друг.** (Here is a friend.)

### Літера П — The Letter П
The letter **П** looks like a doorway. It makes the sound of the English "p". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — П](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JksSjjxyW5Y)

In English, we release a small puff of air for "p". The Ukrainian **П** is unaspirated (no puff of air).

* **паву́к** (spider)
* **парк** (park)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **спорт** (sport)
* **суп** (soup)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там парк.** (There is a park.)
* **Тут по́шта.** (Here is a post office.)

> [!warning]
> **Обережно — Visual Trap Alert!**
> You learned **Р** previously, which looks like an English "P" but sounds like "r". Now you learn **П**, which sounds like "p" but looks like a platform. 
> Compare **па́лка** (stick) with **раке́та** (rocket). 

## Приголосні — Consonants З, Г, Х

### Літера З — The Letter З
The letter **З** makes the sound of the English "z". It is the voiced partner to **С**. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — З](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BhASNxitC1A)

* **зе́бра** (zebra)
* **зуб** (tooth)
* **звук** (sound)
* **зал** (hall)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там зе́бра.** (There is a zebra.)
* **Тут зал.** (Here is a hall.)

### Літера Г — The Letter Г
The letter **Г** does not make a hard "g" sound like "go". It is a soft, throaty sound, closer to a heavy "h". The hard "g" sound is **Ґ** (coming next lesson).

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Г](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gVnclpSI0DU)

* **гора́** (mountain)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **го́лос** (voice)
* **гу́ба** (lip)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там гора́.** (There is a mountain.)
* **Тут газе́та.** (Here is a newspaper.)

### Літера Х — The Letter Х
The letter **Х** looks like X, but makes a different sound. It is the voiceless partner of **Г**. It sounds similar to Scottish "loch" or German "ach". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Х](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpr58zJSJKc)

* **хліб** (bread)
* **хо́лод** (cold)
* **ха́та** (house)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут хліб.** (Here is bread.)
* **Там ха́та.** (There is a house.)

> [!culture]
> **Традиція — Bread and Salt**
> The word **хліб** (bread) is central to Ukrainian culture. The tradition of **хліб-сіль** (bread and salt) is an ancient symbol of hospitality.

## Дзвінкі та глухі — Voiced and Voiceless Pairs

Place your hand on your throat. Say "z" and hold it: zzzzz. You feel vibration. Switch to "s": sssss. The vibration stops. 
This is the difference between voiced (vibrating) and voiceless (air only) consonants.

### Літера Ж — The Letter Ж
The letter **Ж** makes a voiced sibilant sound. It sounds like the "s" in "measure". Keep your lips rounded.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ж](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dIrGVcqPwqM)

* **жук** (bug)
* **жар** (heat)
* **жа́ба** (frog)
* **журна́л** (magazine)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там жа́ба.** (There is a frog.)
* **Тут журна́л.** (Here is a magazine.)

### Літера Ш — The Letter Ш
The letter **Ш** is the voiceless partner of **Ж**. It sounds like English "sh" in "ship". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ш](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1D-6MIw3OXY)

* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **шар** (layer)
* **шум** (noise)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут шко́ла.** (Here is a school.)
* **Там ша́пка.** (There is a hat.)

### Літера Ч — The Letter Ч
The letter **Ч** looks like a small chair. It makes the sound of the English "ch" in "church". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ч](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UsJkbdsY2RA)

* **черепа́ха** (turtle)
* **чай** (tea)
* **час** (time)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут чай.** (Here is tea.)
* **Там черепа́ха.** (There is a turtle.)

> [!tip]
> **Конспект — Consonant Pairs Summary**
> * **Б** (voiced) ↔ **П** (voiceless)
> * **Д** (voiced) ↔ **Т** (voiceless)
> * **Г** (voiced) ↔ **Х** (voiceless)
> * **Ж** (voiced) ↔ **Ш** (voiceless)
> * **З** (voiced) ↔ **С** (voiceless)

## Практика читання — Reading Practice

We will start with internationalisms. 

Read these words aloud:
* **банк** (bank)
* **парк** (park)
* **тра́нспорт** (transport)

Next, discrimination drills. 
Voiced **З** with voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) 
* **суп** (soup) 

Voiced **Ж** with voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat)
* **шар** (layer)

Finally, read some practical vocabulary:
* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
**Там молоко́ і чай.** (There is milk and tea.)
**Тут кіт і соба́ка.** (Here is a cat and a dog.)

## Підсумок — Summary

You just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters. You can read nearly all common text. You also mastered voiced and voiceless pairs. The next step is The Cyrillic Code IV.

**Самоперевірка — Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform ("p" sound). Р looks like a "P" ("r" sound).
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

---

* **Тут зал.** (Here is a hall.)

### Літера Г — The Letter Г
The letter **Г** does not make a hard "g" sound like "go". It is a soft, throaty sound, closer to a heavy "h". The hard "g" sound is **Ґ** (coming next lesson).

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Г](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gVnclpSI0DU)

* **гора́** (mountain)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **го́лос** (voice)
* **гу́ба** (lip)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там гора́.** (There is a mountain.)
* **Тут газе́та.** (Here is a newspaper.)

### Літера Х — The Letter Х
The letter **Х** looks like X, but makes a different sound. It is the voiceless partner of **Г**. It sounds similar to Scottish "loch" or German "ach". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Х](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpr58zJSJKc)

* **хліб** (bread)
* **хо́лод** (cold)
* **ха́та** (house)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут хліб.** (Here is bread.)
* **Там ха́та.** (There is a house.)

> [!culture]
> **Традиція — Bread and Salt**
> The word **хліб** (bread) is central to Ukrainian culture. The tradition of **хліб-сіль** (bread and salt) is an ancient symbol of hospitality.

## Дзвінкі та глухі — Voiced and Voiceless Pairs

Place your hand on your throat. Say "z" and hold it: zzzzz. You feel vibration. Switch to "s": sssss. The vibration stops. 
This is the difference between voiced (vibrating) and voiceless (air only) consonants.

### Літера Ж — The Letter Ж
The letter **Ж** makes a voiced sibilant sound. It sounds like the "s" in "measure". Keep your lips rounded.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ж](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dIrGVcqPwqM)

* **жук** (bug)
* **жар** (heat)
* **жа́ба** (frog)
* **журна́л** (magazine)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там жа́ба.** (There is a frog.)
* **Тут журна́л.** (Here is a magazine.)

### Літера Ш — The Letter Ш
The letter **Ш** is the voiceless partner of **Ж**. It sounds like English "sh" in "ship". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ш](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1D-6MIw3OXY)

* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **шар** (layer)
* **шум** (noise)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут шко́ла.** (Here is a school.)
* **Там ша́пка.** (There is a hat.)

### Літера Ч — The Letter Ч
The letter **Ч** looks like a small chair. It makes the sound of the English "ch" in "church". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ч](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UsJkbdsY2RA)

* **черепа́ха** (turtle)
* **чай** (tea)
* **час** (time)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут чай.** (Here is tea.)
* **Там черепа́ха.** (There is a turtle.)

> [!tip]
> **Конспект — Consonant Pairs Summary**
> * **Б** (voiced) ↔ **П** (voiceless)
> * **Д** (voiced) ↔ **Т** (voiceless)
> * **Г** (voiced) ↔ **Х** (voiceless)
> * **Ж** (voiced) ↔ **Ш** (voiceless)
> * **З** (voiced) ↔ **С** (voiceless)

## Практика читання — Reading Practice

We will start with internationalisms. 

Read these words aloud:
* **банк** (bank)
* **парк** (park)
* **тра́нспорт** (transport)

Next, discrimination drills. 
Voiced **З** with voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) 
* **суп** (soup) 

Voiced **Ж** with voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat)
* **шар** (layer)

Finally, read some practical vocabulary:
* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
**Там молоко́ і чай.** (There is milk and tea.)
**Тут кіт і соба́ка.** (Here is a cat and a dog.)

## Підсумок — Summary

You just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters. You can read nearly all common text. You also mastered voiced and voiceless pairs. The next step is The Cyrillic Code IV.

**Самоперевірка — Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform ("p" sound). Р looks like a "P" ("r" sound).
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

<!-- SCOPE
Covers: 9 new consonants (Б, Д, П, З, Г, Х, Ж, Ш, Ч), voiced and voiceless pairs
Not covered:
  - Remaining 10 letters and digraphs → a1-04 (The Cyrillic Code IV)
-->

# The Cyrillic Code III

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are about to unlock nine new consonant letters. Once you finish this lesson, you will know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. This means nearly all common Ukrainian text will become readable to you.

## Вступ — Introduction

Welcome back! Before we dive into new letters, let's warm up by reading a few words you already know from our previous lessons.

Look at these words and read them out loud:
* **молоко́** (milk)
* **кіт** (cat)
* **мі́сто** (city)
* **соба́ка** (dog)
* **вода́** (water)

Excellent job. You are now ready for the next big step in decoding Ukrainian text. Our main objective for this lesson is to introduce nine new consonant letters: **Б**, **Д**, **П**, **З**, **Г**, **Х**, **Ж**, **Ш**, and **Ч**. Adding these to your toolkit unlocks a massive amount of everyday vocabulary. 

As we explore these new consonants, we will also introduce a key concept in Ukrainian pronunciation called voiced and voiceless consonant pairs. This simply means that Ukrainian consonants often come in matched pairs. One letter in the pair vibrates your throat when you speak it, which we call voiced, while its partner only uses air without vibration, which we call voiceless. Understanding this pattern makes pronouncing new words much easier and more natural.

## Приголосні — Consonants Б, Д, П

Let's start with three simple consonants. These shapes might look slightly unfamiliar, but the sounds they make are very straightforward.

### The Letter Б
The letter **Б** makes the sound of the English "b". It is a voiced consonant.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Б](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1hxBE_JbGg)

Here are some examples of words starting with **Б**:
* **бана́н** (banana)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **брат** (brother)
* **банк** (bank)
* **ба́за** (base)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там бана́н.** (There is a banana.)
* **Тут буди́нок.** (Here is a building.)
* **Там брат.** (There is a brother.)

### The Letter Д
The letter **Д** looks a bit like a house sitting on small legs. It makes the sound of the English "d". In Ukrainian, this sound is dental, meaning you place your tongue against the back of your upper teeth, rather than the roof of your mouth.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Д](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4Bh-lqzd48)

Practice the letter **Д** with these words:
* **дім** (house or home)
* **до́бре** (good)
* **друг** (friend)
* **день** (day)
* **дуб** (oak)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там дім.** (There is a home.)
* **Тут друг.** (Here is a friend.)

### The Letter П
The letter **П** looks like a platform or a doorway. It makes the sound of the English "p". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — П](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JksSjjxyW5Y)

There is a very important detail here. In English, when we say a word starting with "p", we usually release a small puff of air. The Ukrainian **П** is unaspirated. This means you should try to say it without that puff of air.

Here are some common words:
* **паву́к** (spider)
* **парк** (park)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **спорт** (sport)
* **суп** (soup)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там парк.** (There is a park.)
* **Тут по́шта.** (Here is a post office.)

> [!warning]
> **Visual Trap Alert!**
> You learned the letter **Р** previously, which looks like an English "P" but sounds like an "r". Now you are learning **П**, which sounds like a "p" but looks like a platform. Do not confuse them!
> Compare **па́лка** (stick) with **раке́та** (rocket). The first word starts with the "p" sound, while the second starts with the "r" sound. 

## Приголосні — Consonants З, Г, Х

Now we will look at three more consonants that require a bit more attention to how they are produced in the mouth and throat.

### The Letter З
The letter **З** looks just like the number three. It makes the sound of the English "z". It is the voiced partner to the letter **С**, which you already know. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — З](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BhASNxitC1A)

Try reading these words:
* **зе́бра** (zebra)
* **зуб** (tooth)
* **звук** (sound)
* **зал** (hall)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там зе́бра.** (There is a zebra.)
* **Тут зал.** (Here is a hall.)

### The Letter Г
The letter **Г** looks like an upside-down L. This letter causes a lot of confusion for beginners, so pay close attention. It does not make a hard "g" sound like in the English word "go". 

Instead, the Ukrainian **Г** is a soft, throaty sound. It is a voiced glottal fricative, sounding a bit closer to a heavy "h". The hard "g" sound does exist in Ukrainian, but it is represented by a different letter, **Ґ**, which we will cover in the next lesson.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Г](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gVnclpSI0DU)

Practice this soft, throaty sound:
* **гора́** (mountain)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **го́лос** (voice)
* **гу́ба** (lip)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там гора́.** (There is a mountain.)
* **Тут газе́та.** (Here is a newspaper.)

### The Letter Х
The letter **Х** looks like the English X, but it makes a completely different sound. It is the voiceless partner of **Г**. 

To pronounce it, you need to create friction in the back of your throat. It sounds similar to the Scottish "loch" or the German "ach". There is no exact English equivalent, so it requires explicit practice. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Х](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpr58zJSJKc)

Read this very important cultural word:
* **хліб** (bread)
* **хо́лод** (cold)
* **ха́та** (house)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Тут хліб.** (Here is bread.)
* **Там ха́та.** (There is a house.)

> [!culture]
> **Bread and Salt**
> The word **хліб** (bread) is central to Ukrainian culture. The tradition of **хліб-сіль** (bread and salt) is an ancient symbol of hospitality. Guests are often welcomed with a loaf of bread topped with salt, showing respect and friendship.

## Дзвінкі та глухі — Voiced and Voiceless Pairs

We have mentioned voiced and voiceless pairs briefly. Now, let's explore this concept practically. 

Place your hand flat against the front of your throat. Say the English sound "z" and hold it: zzzzz. You should feel a strong vibration in your throat. Now, switch to the "s" sound: sssss. The vibration stops, and you only feel air flowing. 

This physical test shows you the difference between a voiced consonant and a voiceless consonant. In Ukrainian, many consonants are organized into these exact pairs. When you learn one, you essentially learn how to form the other by just turning your voice on or off.

Let's learn the final three consonants for this lesson. They are known as sibilants and affricates, which are just technical terms for "hissing" and "buzzing" sounds.

### The Letter Ж
The letter **Ж** looks like a little bug with six legs. It makes a voiced sibilant sound. There is no single letter in English for this, but it sounds like the "s" in "measure" or the "z" in "azure". Keep your lips slightly rounded and your tongue raised.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ж](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dIrGVcqPwqM)

* **жук** (bug)
* **жар** (heat)
* **жа́ба** (frog)
* **журна́л** (magazine)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Там жа́ба.** (There is a frog.)
* **Тут журна́л.** (Here is a magazine.)

### The Letter Ш
The letter **Ш** looks like a comb pointing upwards. It is the voiceless partner of **Ж**. It sounds very much like the English "sh" in "ship". When you say it, your tongue is pulled back slightly for a hard articulation.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ш](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1D-6MIw3OXY)

* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **шар** (layer)
* **шум** (noise)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Тут шко́ла.** (Here is a school.)
* **Там ша́пка.** (There is a hat.)

### The Letter Ч
The letter **Ч** looks like an upside-down lower-case "h" or a small chair. It makes the sound of the English "ch" in "church". This is a very frequent sound in Ukrainian.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ч](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UsJkbdsY2RA)

* **черепа́ха** (turtle)
* **чай** (tea)
* **час** (time)

Let's read some short phrases:
* **Тут чай.** (Here is tea.)
* **Там черепа́ха.** (There is a turtle.)

> [!tip]
> **Consonant Pairs Summary**
> Let's summarize the pairs you now know. Practice saying them aloud, feeling your throat turn on and off:
> * **Б** (voiced) ↔ **П** (voiceless)
> * **Д** (voiced) ↔ **Т** (voiceless)
> * **Г** (voiced) ↔ **Х** (voiceless)
> * **Ж** (voiced) ↔ **Ш** (voiceless)
> * **З** (voiced) ↔ **С** (voiceless)

## Практика читання — Reading Practice

You have absorbed a lot of new information. The best way to secure it in your memory is to practice reading. We will start with words that are internationalisms. These words sound very similar to their English counterparts, which will build your immediate confidence in recognizing the new letters.

Read these words aloud:
* **банк** (bank)
* **парк** (park)
* **тра́нспорт** (transport)

Next, we will do some discrimination drills. These pairs of words look similar but use different consonants. Pay attention to whether your throat is vibrating or not.

First, let's compare the voiced **З** with the voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) — voiced start
* **суп** (soup) — voiceless start

Now, let's compare the voiced **Ж** with the voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat) — voiced start
* **шар** (layer) — voiceless start

Finally, let's read some highly practical everyday vocabulary. These words use letters from the entire pool of twenty-three characters you have learned so far. Take your time and decode them letter by letter.

* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence of sentences.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
**Там молоко́ і чай.** (There is milk and tea.)
**Тут кіт і соба́ка.** (Here is a cat and a dog.)

You are doing wonderfully. Every word you read makes the letters more familiar to your brain.

## Підсумок — Summary

Congratulations! You have just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three out of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet. This includes six vowels and seventeen consonants. You can now read nearly all common text you will encounter in everyday situations. Furthermore, you have mastered the key concept of voiced and voiceless pairs, which will help your pronunciation immensely. 

The next step in your journey will be The Cyrillic Code IV, where we will complete the alphabet by learning the remaining ten letters and special symbols.

**Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform and makes a "p" sound. Р looks like a "P" but makes an "r" sound.
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

# The Cyrillic Code III

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are about to unlock nine new consonant letters. Once you finish this lesson, you will know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet.

## Вступ — Introduction

Welcome back! Let's warm up by reading a few words you already know.

Read them out loud:
* **молоко́** (milk)
* **кіт** (cat)
* **мі́сто** (city)
* **соба́ка** (dog)
* **вода́** (water)

Our objective is to introduce nine new consonant letters: **Б**, **Д**, **П**, **З**, **Г**, **Х**, **Ж**, **Ш**, and **Ч**. Adding these unlocks a massive amount of vocabulary. 

We will also introduce voiced and voiceless consonant pairs. One letter vibrates your throat (voiced), while its partner uses only air (voiceless).

## Приголосні — Consonants Б, Д, П

Let's start with three simple consonants. 

### Літера Б — The Letter Б
The letter **Б** makes the sound of the English "b". It is a voiced consonant.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Б](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1hxBE_JbGg)

Examples of words starting with **Б**:
* **бана́н** (banana)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **брат** (brother)
* **банк** (bank)
* **ба́за** (base)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там бана́н.** (There is a banana.)
* **Тут буди́нок.** (Here is a building.)
* **Там брат.** (There is a brother.)

### Літера Д — The Letter Д
The letter **Д** looks a bit like a house. It makes the sound of the English "d". In Ukrainian, this sound is dental (tongue against teeth).

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Д](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4Bh-lqzd48)

Practice the letter **Д**:
* **дім** (house or home)
* **до́бре** (good)
* **друг** (friend)
* **день** (day)
* **дуб** (oak)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там дім.** (There is a home.)
* **Тут друг.** (Here is a friend.)

### Літера П — The Letter П
The letter **П** looks like a doorway. It makes the sound of the English "p". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — П](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JksSjjxyW5Y)

In English, we release a small puff of air for "p". The Ukrainian **П** is unaspirated (no puff of air).

* **паву́к** (spider)
* **парк** (park)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **спорт** (sport)
* **суп** (soup)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там парк.** (There is a park.)
* **Тут по́шта.** (Here is a post office.)

> [!warning]
> **Обережно — Visual Trap Alert!**
> You learned **Р** previously, which looks like an English "P" but sounds like "r". Now you learn **П**, which sounds like "p" but looks like a platform. 
> Compare **па́лка** (stick) with **раке́та** (rocket). 

## Приголосні — Consonants З, Г, Х

### Літера З — The Letter З
The letter **З** makes the sound of the English "z". It is the voiced partner to **С**. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — З](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BhASNxitC1A)

* **зе́бра** (zebra)
* **зуб** (tooth)
* **звук** (sound)
* **зал** (hall)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там зе́бра.** (There is a zebra.)
* **Тут зал.** (Here is a hall.)

### Літера Г — The Letter Г
The letter **Г** does not make a hard "g" sound like "go". It is a soft, throaty sound, closer to a heavy "h". The hard "g" sound is **Ґ** (coming next lesson).

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Г](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gVnclpSI0DU)

* **гора́** (mountain)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **го́лос** (voice)
* **гу́ба** (lip)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там гора́.** (There is a mountain.)
* **Тут газе́та.** (Here is a newspaper.)

### Літера Х — The Letter Х
The letter **Х** looks like X, but makes a different sound. It is the voiceless partner of **Г**. It sounds similar to Scottish "loch" or German "ach". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Х](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpr58zJSJKc)

* **хліб** (bread)
* **хо́лод** (cold)
* **ха́та** (house)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут хліб.** (Here is bread.)
* **Там ха́та.** (There is a house.)

> [!culture]
> **Традиція — Bread and Salt**
> The word **хліб** (bread) is central to Ukrainian culture. The tradition of **хліб-сіль** (bread and salt) is an ancient symbol of hospitality.

## Дзвінкі та глухі — Voiced and Voiceless Pairs

Place your hand on your throat. Say "z" and hold it: zzzzz. You feel vibration. Switch to "s": sssss. The vibration stops. 
This is the difference between voiced (vibrating) and voiceless (air only) consonants.

### Літера Ж — The Letter Ж
The letter **Ж** makes a voiced sibilant sound. It sounds like the "s" in "measure". Keep your lips rounded.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ж](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dIrGVcqPwqM)

* **жук** (bug)
* **жар** (heat)
* **жа́ба** (frog)
* **журна́л** (magazine)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там жа́ба.** (There is a frog.)
* **Тут журна́л.** (Here is a magazine.)

### Літера Ш — The Letter Ш
The letter **Ш** is the voiceless partner of **Ж**. It sounds like English "sh" in "ship". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ш](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1D-6MIw3OXY)

* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **шар** (layer)
* **шум** (noise)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут шко́ла.** (Here is a school.)
* **Там ша́пка.** (There is a hat.)

### Літера Ч — The Letter Ч
The letter **Ч** looks like a small chair. It makes the sound of the English "ch" in "church". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ч](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UsJkbdsY2RA)

* **черепа́ха** (turtle)
* **чай** (tea)
* **час** (time)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут чай.** (Here is tea.)
* **Там черепа́ха.** (There is a turtle.)

> [!tip]
> **Конспект — Consonant Pairs Summary**
> * **Б** (voiced) ↔ **П** (voiceless)
> * **Д** (voiced) ↔ **Т** (voiceless)
> * **Г** (voiced) ↔ **Х** (voiceless)
> * **Ж** (voiced) ↔ **Ш** (voiceless)
> * **З** (voiced) ↔ **С** (voiceless)

## Практика читання — Reading Practice

We will start with internationalisms. 

Read these words aloud:
* **банк** (bank)
* **парк** (park)
* **тра́нспорт** (transport)

Next, discrimination drills. 
Voiced **З** with voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) 
* **суп** (soup) 

Voiced **Ж** with voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat)
* **шар** (layer)

Finally, read some practical vocabulary:
* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
**Там молоко́ і чай.** (There is milk and tea.)
**Тут кіт і соба́ка.** (Here is a cat and a dog.)

## Підсумок — Summary

You just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters. You can read nearly all common text. You also mastered voiced and voiceless pairs. The next step is The Cyrillic Code IV.

**Самоперевірка — Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform ("p" sound). Р looks like a "P" ("r" sound).
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. To run a command in the background, set the `is_background` parameter to true. Do NOT use `&` to background commands. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group. Command process group can be terminated as `kill -- -PGID` or signaled as `kill -s SIGNAL -- -PGID`.

      Efficiency Guidelines:
      - Quiet Flags: Always prefer silent or quiet flags (e.g., `npm install --silent`, `git --no-pager`) to reduce output volume while still capturing necessary information.
      - Pagination: Always disable terminal pagination to ensure commands terminate (e.g., use `git --no-pager`, `systemctl --no-pager`, or set `PAGER=cat`).

      The following information is returned:

      Output: Combined stdout/stderr. Can be `(empty)` or partial on error and for any unwaited background processes.
      Exit Code: Only included if non-zero (command failed).
      Error: Only included if a process-level error occurred (e.g., spawn failure).
      Signal: Only included if process was terminated by a signal.
      Background PIDs: Only included if background processes were started.
      Process Group PGID: Only included if available.

args.content:
<!-- SCOPE
Covers: 9 new consonants (Б, Д, П, З, Г, Х, Ж, Ш, Ч), voiced and voiceless pairs
Not covered:
  - Remaining 10 letters and digraphs → a1-04 (The Cyrillic Code IV)
-->

# The Cyrillic Code III

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are about to unlock nine new consonant letters. Once you finish this lesson, you will know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet.

## Вступ — Introduction

Welcome back to your Cyrillic decoding journey! Before we dive into learning new symbols, let's take a moment to warm up by reviewing and reading a few familiar vocabulary items from our previous lessons. Seeing words you already know will boost your confidence.

Read these words out loud slowly:
* **молоко́** (milk)
* **кіт** (cat)
* **мі́сто** (city)
* **соба́ка** (dog)
* **вода́** (water)

Excellent job recognizing those shapes. You are now fully prepared for the next big step in decoding Ukrainian text. Our primary objective for this particular lesson is to introduce nine new consonant characters: **Б**, **Д**, **П**, **З**, **Г**, **Х**, **Ж**, **Ш**, and **Ч**. Adding these specific characters to your reading toolkit will unlock a massive amount of everyday vocabulary and signage.

As we explore these new consonants, we will also introduce a vital concept in Ukrainian pronunciation known as voiced and voiceless consonant pairs. This rule simply means that Ukrainian consonants very often come in matched pairs based on how they are produced. One letter in the pair vibrates your vocal cords in your throat (which we call a voiced sound), while its partner uses only pushing air without any vibration (which we call a voiceless sound). Grasping this pattern makes pronouncing unfamiliar new words much easier, more predictable, and significantly more natural.

## Приголосні — Consonants Б, Д, П

Let's start with three simple consonants. 

### Літера Б — The Letter Б
The letter **Б** produces the sound of an English "b". This is a voiced consonant.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Б](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1hxBE_JbGg)

Examples of words starting with **Б**:
* **бана́н** (banana)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **брат** (brother)
* **банк** (bank)
* **ба́за** (base)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там бана́н.** (There is a banana.)
* **Тут буди́нок.** (Here is a building.)
* **Там брат.** (There is a brother.)

### Літера Д — The Letter Д
The character **Д** resembles a little house resting on small legs. It corresponds to the English "d", but the Ukrainian version is dental. This means you must place your tongue against the back of your upper teeth.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Д](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4Bh-lqzd48)

Practice the letter **Д**:
* **дім** (house or home)
* **до́бре** (good)
* **друг** (friend)
* **день** (day)
* **дуб** (oak)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там дім.** (There is a home.)
* **Тут друг.** (Here is a friend.)

### Літера П — The Letter П
You will notice that **П** looks quite like a wide doorway. It represents the "p" sound. Remember that in English, we usually release a small puff of air for this letter, but the Ukrainian **П** is unaspirated. Do your best to avoid that extra breath.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — П](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JksSjjxyW5Y)

* **паву́к** (spider)
* **парк** (park)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **спорт** (sport)
* **суп** (soup)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там парк.** (There is a park.)
* **Тут по́шта.** (Here is a post office.)

> [!warning]
> **Обережно — Visual Trap Alert!**
> You learned **Р** previously, which looks like an English "P" but sounds like "r". Now you learn **П**, which sounds like "p" but looks like a platform. 
> Compare **па́лка** (stick) with **раке́та** (rocket). Watch out!

## Приголосні — Consonants З, Г, Х

### Літера З — The Letter З
When you see the symbol **З**, you might think it is the number three. Functionally, it stands for the "z" noise and serves as the voiced counterpart to **С**. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — З](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BhASNxitC1A)

* **зе́бра** (zebra)
* **зуб** (tooth)
* **звук** (sound)
* **зал** (hall)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там зе́бра.** (There is a zebra.)
* **Тут зал.** (Here is a hall.)

### Літера Г — The Letter Г
The letter **Г** looks somewhat like an upside-down L. This character frequently causes confusion for beginners, so pay close attention. It does not map to a hard English "go" sound. Instead, the Ukrainian **Г** is a soft, throaty glottal fricative, landing closer to a heavy "h". The hard "g" sound does exist, but uses a different shape (**Ґ**).

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Г](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gVnclpSI0DU)

* **гора́** (mountain)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **го́лос** (voice)
* **гу́ба** (lip)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там гора́.** (There is a mountain.)
* **Тут газе́та.** (Here is a newspaper.)

### Літера Х — The Letter Х
Even though **Х** looks identical to the English X, it produces a totally different sound. It acts as the voiceless partner of **Г**. To pronounce it properly, create some friction in the back of your throat, similar to the Scottish "loch" or the German "ach". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Х](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpr58zJSJKc)

* **хліб** (bread)
* **хо́лод** (cold)
* **ха́та** (house)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут хліб.** (Here is bread.)
* **Там ха́та.** (There is a house.)

> [!culture]
> **Традиція — Bread and Salt**
> The word **хліб** (bread) is central to Ukrainian culture. The tradition of **хліб-сіль** (bread and salt) is an ancient symbol of hospitality.

## Дзвінкі та глухі — Voiced and Voiceless Pairs

Place your hand flat against the front of your throat. Say the sound "z" and hold it: zzzzz. You should feel a strong vibration. Now, immediately switch to the "s" sound: sssss. The vibration stops entirely, leaving only air flowing. 
This physical test perfectly demonstrates the difference between voiced (vibrating) and voiceless (air only) consonants. Many Ukrainian letters pair up this exact way.

### Літера Ж — The Letter Ж
The shape **Ж** resembles a little bug. It delivers a voiced sibilant sound. Think of the "s" in "measure" or the "z" in "azure". Keep your lips slightly rounded and your tongue raised toward the roof of your mouth.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ж](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dIrGVcqPwqM)

* **жук** (bug)
* **жар** (heat)
* **жа́ба** (frog)
* **журна́л** (magazine)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там жа́ба.** (There is a frog.)
* **Тут журна́л.** (Here is a magazine.)

### Літера Ш — The Letter Ш
You will spot **Ш** looking like a comb pointing upwards. It functions as the voiceless partner of **Ж**. It sounds extremely similar to the English "sh" in "ship". When articulating it, pull your tongue back slightly.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ш](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1D-6MIw3OXY)

* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **шар** (layer)
* **шум** (noise)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут шко́ла.** (Here is a school.)
* **Там ша́пка.** (There is a hat.)

### Літера Ч — The Letter Ч
The letter **Ч** appears somewhat like an upside-down lower-case "h" or perhaps a small chair. It creates the sound of the English "ch" in "church". You will encounter this very frequently.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ч](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UsJkbdsY2RA)

* **черепа́ха** (turtle)
* **чай** (tea)
* **час** (time)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут чай.** (Here is tea.)
* **Там черепа́ха.** (There is a turtle.)

> [!tip]
> **Конспект — Consonant Pairs Summary**
> * **Б** (voiced) ↔ **П** (voiceless)
> * **Д** (voiced) ↔ **Т** (voiceless)
> * **Г** (voiced) ↔ **Х** (voiceless)
> * **Ж** (voiced) ↔ **Ш** (voiceless)
> * **З** (voiced) ↔ **С** (voiceless)

## Практика читання — Reading Practice

We will start our reading drill with internationalisms. These specific vocabulary items sound very similar to their English counterparts. Practicing with these will build your immediate confidence in recognizing the newly acquired letters within real contexts.

Read these familiar-sounding words aloud:
* **банк** (bank)
* **парк** (park)
* **тра́нспорт** (transport)
* **спорт** (sport)
* **суп** (soup)

Next, we will do some targeted discrimination drills. These word pairs look visually similar but use distinctly different consonants. Pay close attention to whether your throat is actively vibrating or completely still.

Compare the voiced **З** with the voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) — voiced start
* **суп** (soup) — voiceless start

Now, compare the voiced **Ж** with the voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat) — voiced start
* **шар** (layer) — voiceless start

Finally, let's carefully read some highly practical everyday vocabulary. These items use letters drawn from the entire pool of twenty-three characters you have learned so far. Take your time, break them down, and decode them character by character.

* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence of descriptive sentences to bring everything together.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
**Там молоко́ і чай.** (There is milk and tea.)
**Тут кіт і соба́ка.** (Here is a cat and a dog.)

## Підсумок — Summary

You just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters. You can read nearly all common text. You also mastered voiced and voiceless pairs. The next step is The Cyrillic Code IV.

**Самоперевірка — Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform ("p" sound). Р looks like a "P" ("r" sound).
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

---

* **суп** (soup) — voiceless start

Now, compare the voiced **Ж** with the voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat) — voiced start
* **шар** (layer) — voiceless start

Finally, let's carefully read some highly practical everyday vocabulary. These items use letters drawn from the entire pool of twenty-three characters you have learned so far. Take your time, break them down, and decode them character by character.

* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence of descriptive sentences to bring everything together.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
...

<!-- SCOPE
Covers: 9 new consonants (Б, Д, П, З, Г, Х, Ж, Ш, Ч), voiced and voiceless pairs
Not covered:
  - Remaining 10 letters and digraphs → a1-04 (The Cyrillic Code IV)
-->

# The Cyrillic Code III

> **Why does this matter?**
>
> You are about to unlock nine new consonant letters. Once you finish this lesson, you will know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters in the Ukrainian alphabet.

## Вступ — Introduction

Welcome back! Let's warm up by reading a few words you already know.

Read them out loud:
* **молоко́** (milk)
* **кіт** (cat)
* **мі́сто** (city)
* **соба́ка** (dog)
* **вода́** (water)

Our objective is to introduce nine new consonant letters: **Б**, **Д**, **П**, **З**, **Г**, **Х**, **Ж**, **Ш**, and **Ч**. Adding these unlocks a massive amount of vocabulary. 

We will also introduce voiced and voiceless consonant pairs. One letter vibrates your throat (voiced), while its partner uses only air (voiceless).

## Приголосні — Consonants Б, Д, П

Let's start with three simple consonants. 

### Літера Б — The Letter Б
The letter **Б** makes the sound of the English "b". It is a voiced consonant.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Б](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1hxBE_JbGg)

Examples of words starting with **Б**:
* **бана́н** (banana)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **брат** (brother)
* **банк** (bank)
* **ба́за** (base)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там бана́н.** (There is a banana.)
* **Тут буди́нок.** (Here is a building.)
* **Там брат.** (There is a brother.)

### Літера Д — The Letter Д
The letter **Д** looks a bit like a house. It makes the sound of the English "d". In Ukrainian, this sound is dental (tongue against teeth).

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Д](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=g4Bh-lqzd48)

Practice the letter **Д**:
* **дім** (house or home)
* **до́бре** (good)
* **друг** (friend)
* **день** (day)
* **дуб** (oak)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там дім.** (There is a home.)
* **Тут друг.** (Here is a friend.)

### Літера П — The Letter П
The letter **П** looks like a doorway. It makes the sound of the English "p". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — П](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JksSjjxyW5Y)

In English, we release a small puff of air for "p". The Ukrainian **П** is unaspirated (no puff of air).

* **паву́к** (spider)
* **парк** (park)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **спорт** (sport)
* **суп** (soup)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там парк.** (There is a park.)
* **Тут по́шта.** (Here is a post office.)

> [!warning]
> **Обережно — Visual Trap Alert!**
> You learned **Р** previously, which looks like an English "P" but sounds like "r". Now you learn **П**, which sounds like "p" but looks like a platform. 
> Compare **па́лка** (stick) with **раке́та** (rocket). 

## Приголосні — Consonants З, Г, Х

### Літера З — The Letter З
The letter **З** makes the sound of the English "z". It is the voiced partner to **С**. 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — З](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BhASNxitC1A)

* **зе́бра** (zebra)
* **зуб** (tooth)
* **звук** (sound)
* **зал** (hall)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там зе́бра.** (There is a zebra.)
* **Тут зал.** (Here is a hall.)

### Літера Г — The Letter Г
The letter **Г** does not make a hard "g" sound like "go". It is a soft, throaty sound, closer to a heavy "h". The hard "g" sound is **Ґ** (coming next lesson).

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Г](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gVnclpSI0DU)

* **гора́** (mountain)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **го́лос** (voice)
* **гу́ба** (lip)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там гора́.** (There is a mountain.)
* **Тут газе́та.** (Here is a newspaper.)

### Літера Х — The Letter Х
The letter **Х** looks like X, but makes a different sound. It is the voiceless partner of **Г**. It sounds similar to Scottish "loch" or German "ach". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Х](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vpr58zJSJKc)

* **хліб** (bread)
* **хо́лод** (cold)
* **ха́та** (house)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут хліб.** (Here is bread.)
* **Там ха́та.** (There is a house.)

> [!culture]
> **Традиція — Bread and Salt**
> The word **хліб** (bread) is central to Ukrainian culture. The tradition of **хліб-сіль** (bread and salt) is an ancient symbol of hospitality.

## Дзвінкі та глухі — Voiced and Voiceless Pairs

Place your hand on your throat. Say "z" and hold it: zzzzz. You feel vibration. Switch to "s": sssss. The vibration stops. 
This is the difference between voiced (vibrating) and voiceless (air only) consonants.

### Літера Ж — The Letter Ж
The letter **Ж** makes a voiced sibilant sound. It sounds like the "s" in "measure". Keep your lips rounded.

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ж](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dIrGVcqPwqM)

* **жук** (bug)
* **жар** (heat)
* **жа́ба** (frog)
* **журна́л** (magazine)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Там жа́ба.** (There is a frog.)
* **Тут журна́л.** (Here is a magazine.)

### Літера Ш — The Letter Ш
The letter **Ш** is the voiceless partner of **Ж**. It sounds like English "sh" in "ship". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ш](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=1D-6MIw3OXY)

* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **шар** (layer)
* **шум** (noise)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут шко́ла.** (Here is a school.)
* **Там ша́пка.** (There is a hat.)

### Літера Ч — The Letter Ч
The letter **Ч** looks like a small chair. It makes the sound of the English "ch" in "church". 

[Anna Ohoiko — Ukrainian Lessons — Ч](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UsJkbdsY2RA)

* **черепа́ха** (turtle)
* **чай** (tea)
* **час** (time)

**Коро́ткі фра́зи — Short phrases:**
* **Тут чай.** (Here is tea.)
* **Там черепа́ха.** (There is a turtle.)

> [!tip]
> **Конспект — Consonant Pairs Summary**
> * **Б** (voiced) ↔ **П** (voiceless)
> * **Д** (voiced) ↔ **Т** (voiceless)
> * **Г** (voiced) ↔ **Х** (voiceless)
> * **Ж** (voiced) ↔ **Ш** (voiceless)
> * **З** (voiced) ↔ **С** (voiceless)

## Практика читання — Reading Practice

We will start with internationalisms. 

Read these words aloud:
* **банк** (bank)
* **парк** (park)
* **тра́нспорт** (transport)

Next, discrimination drills. 
Voiced **З** with voiceless **С**:
* **зуб** (tooth) 
* **суп** (soup) 

Voiced **Ж** with voiceless **Ш**:
* **жар** (heat)
* **шар** (layer)

Finally, read some practical vocabulary:
* **хліб** (bread)
* **шко́ла** (school)
* **буди́нок** (building)
* **газе́та** (newspaper)
* **по́шта** (post office)
* **ша́пка** (hat)
* **чай** (tea)

**Коро́ткий текст — Short text:**
Read this short sequence.
**Тут мі́сто.** (Here is a city.)
**Там парк.** (There is a park.)
**Тут шко́ла і буди́нок.** (Here is a school and a building.)
**Там магази́н.** (There is a store.)
**Тут хліб і вода́.** (Here is bread and water.)
**Там молоко́ і чай.** (There is milk and tea.)
**Тут кіт і соба́ка.** (Here is a cat and a dog.)

## Підсумок — Summary

You just unlocked nine crucial consonants. You now know twenty-three of the thirty-three letters. You can read nearly all common text. You also mastered voiced and voiceless pairs. The next step is The Cyrillic Code IV.

**Самоперевірка — Self-Check:**
1. Чи відчуваєте ви вібрацію в горлі між Б і П? (Can you feel the difference in your throat between Б and П?) — Б is voiced (vibrates), П is voiceless.
2. Яка візуальна різниця між літерами П і Р? (What is the visual difference between the letters П and Р?) — П looks like a platform ("p" sound). Р looks like a "P" ("r" sound).
3. Як ви вимовляєте Г і Х? (How do you pronounce Г vs Х?) — Г is soft and throaty (voiced), while Х is a voiceless friction sound like "ach".

---
